title: Karachi Style Fish Fry

seo.title: Karachi Style Fish Fry: Authentic Pakistani Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of Karachi Style Fish Fry with our authentic Pakistani recipe! Get step-by-step instructions for a crispy, flavorful seafood delight. Perfect for any meal.

seo.imageAlt: A plate of crispy, golden-brown Karachi Style Fish Fry garnished with lemon wedges and fresh herbs, ready to be served.
seo.imageCaption: Indulge in the crispy, spicy goodness of our authentic Karachi Style Fish Fry – a true taste of Pakistan!

description: Karachi Style Fish Fry is a beloved Pakistani seafood delight known for its crispy exterior and tender, flavorful interior. This iconic dish features fish marinated in a blend of aromatic spices and then deep-fried to golden perfection. It's a culinary staple from the bustling streets of Karachi, offering a truly authentic taste experience.

ingredients.notes: e.g., Kingfish, Pomfret, Cod, cut into 2-inch pieces

instructions.title: Prepare the Fish
instructions.text: Wash fish pieces thoroughly and pat them completely dry with paper towels. Make shallow slits on thicker pieces to help with marination. In a bowl, add the fish, 2 tablespoons of lemon juice, and a pinch of salt. Mix gently and let it rest for 10 minutes.

instructions.title: First Marinade
instructions.text: Drain any excess water from the fish. In a large mixing bowl, combine ginger-garlic paste, red chili powder, turmeric powder, coriander powder, cumin powder, garam masala powder, crushed ajwain, and the remaining 2 tablespoons of lemon juice. Mix these ingredients well to form a thick, aromatic paste.

instructions.title: Marinate the Fish
instructions.text: Add the prepared fish pieces to the spice paste. Ensure each piece is thoroughly coated with the marinade. Cover the bowl and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or preferably 2-3 hours, to allow the flavors to deeply penetrate the fish.

instructions.title: Prepare the Coating
instructions.text: In a separate shallow dish, combine besan (gram flour), rice flour, and salt. Gradually add 2-3 tablespoons of water to the mixture, stirring constantly, to create a slightly thick, pourable batter consistency that will lightly coat the fish pieces. This helps achieve a crispy exterior.

instructions.title: Heat Oil and Coat Fish
instructions.text: Heat cooking oil in a deep pan or wok over medium-high heat. The oil should be hot enough for deep frying. To test, drop a small amount of batter into the oil; it should sizzle immediately. Once hot, dip each marinated fish piece into the besan-rice flour batter, ensuring an even coating on all sides.

instructions.title: Deep Frying
instructions.text: Carefully place the coated fish into the hot oil. Avoid overcrowding the pan to maintain oil temperature and ensure even cooking. Fry the fish in batches for about 5-7 minutes per side, or until it turns golden brown, crispy on the outside, and cooked through on the inside. Cooking time may vary based on fish thickness.

instructions.title: Serve Hot
instructions.text: Remove the fried fish from the oil using a slotted spoon and place it on a wire rack or paper towels to drain any excess oil. Sprinkle with a generous amount of chaat masala, garnish with fresh lemon wedges, and serve immediately with a side of mint chutney or raita for an authentic Karachi experience.

tips: For extra crispiness, ensure your fish is completely dry before marinating, and maintain a consistent oil temperature during frying, avoiding overcrowding the pan.
Adjust the amount of red chili powder and green chilies (if adding) according to your spice preference. A sprinkle of chaat masala after frying elevates the flavor.
Serve Karachi Style Fish Fry immediately after frying to enjoy its best texture. It pairs wonderfully with a squeeze of fresh lemon, mint-coriander chutney, or a simple raita.
article: Karachi Style Fish Fry is a beloved Pakistani Seafood dish enjoyed across Pakistan. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
